entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br., the crop studied for yield stability in Senegal

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The production of grain, used as the primary trait for evaluating genotype performance

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: The country where the pearl millet genotypes were grown across four locations for four years

entities.name: GENOTYPE ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between genotypes and their environments, which was studied for grain yield

entities.name: NON-LINEAR REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical method used to account for a large portion of the genotype environment interaction

entities.name: LINEAR COMPONENT
entities.description: A part of the regression analysis that was significant but smaller than the non-linear component

entities.name: SOUNA III
entities.description: A pearl millet genotype that was found to be unstable in its response to environment changes

entities.name: IBV 8001
entities.description: A pearl millet genotype that was the highest yielding and most desirable due to its stability

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of grain yield across different environments, used to evaluate genotypes
